region_of_origin: United States (globally popularised; served in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Processed cheese snack — breaded and deep-fried mozzarella sticks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a mozzarella cheese food product class but no specific class for breaded deep-fried mozzarella sticks. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cheese, processed — used as closest parent for breaded fried mozzarella sticks
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for breaded fried mozzarella sticks. A0EJX (processed cheese) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226760005 (cheese snack) is the closest available parent; no specific code for mozzarella sticks exists in the reviewed release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard order of 12 mozzarella cheese sticks weighs approximately 240 g (about 20 g per stick), representing a typical shared snack portion for 2–4 people.

nutrient_source.title: Mozzarella sticks, frozen, fried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Singapore-specific entry for Potato Corner Mega Mozza Cheese Sticks exists in HPB Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for breaded fried mozzarella sticks (NDB 21052 / similar SR Legacy entries) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for comparable commercial mozzarella stick products. Per-100g values represent a composite estimate for a standard breaded deep-fried mozzarella stick product. Serving size of 240 g (12 × ~20 g sticks) is estimated from typical commercial product weights.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of breaded fried foods — analogous estimate based on white bread-coated fried snack foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for mozzarella cheese sticks exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated by analogy with breaded fried snack foods with a wheat-flour coating, which typically fall in the 50–60 range (low GI, as 55 is <= 55). The cheese filling itself has negligible GI contribution. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 56.4) / 100 = 30.02, rounded to 30.0. Estimation basis documented per Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1248 mg per 12-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid mozzarella cheese sticks due to the very high sodium load per serving (~1248 mg, representing over 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg), which can acutely elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Officially low glycemic index (55) but combined with large carbohydrate load per serving (~56 g) and high energy density
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume mozzarella cheese sticks in small portions only. While the glycemic index is officially low (55), the high per-serving carbohydrate load (~56 g) from the breading can still impact blood glucose, and the high fat content contributes to caloric excess and insulin resistance risk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~6.2 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), sodium (~520 mg/100 g), and deep-frying preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should strictly limit mozzarella cheese sticks due to the combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium per serving, all of which are independently associated with elevated LDL cholesterol, arterial inflammation, and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mozzarella cheese sticks are a popular Western-origin deep-fried snack consisting of elongated portions of mozzarella cheese encased in a seasoned wheat-flour breadcrumb coating, then deep-fried in vegetable oil until the exterior is golden and crispy while the interior cheese melts into a stretchy, gooey texture. In Singapore, they are commonly sold at fast-casual Western snack chains such as Potato Corner, where they are offered in multi-piece orders (e.g., 12 pieces) with optional flavour seasonings. Each stick weighs approximately 20 g, giving a 12-piece serving of around 240 g. Nutritionally, mozzarella cheese sticks are energy-dense (~271 kcal/100 g), with a moderate protein content (~12.5 g/100 g) from the cheese, significant fat (~14.8 g/100 g, of which ~6.2 g is saturated), and a meaningful carbohydrate contribution (~23.5 g/100 g) from the breadcrumb coating. Sodium is notably high (~520 mg/100 g), making a full 12-piece serving a high-sodium food. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low) due to the refined wheat coating. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals managing hypertension (very high sodium per serving), cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, sodium), and Type 2 Diabetes (low GI, but high carbohydrate load per serving). It is vegetarian but not vegan, not gluten-free, and not dairy-free. It contains milk and gluten allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in mozzarella cheese sticks?
How much sodium in fried cheese sticks?
Is Potato Corner Mozza Cheese Sticks halal?
What is the GI of mozzarella sticks?
Are mozzarella sticks high in fat?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Potato Corner Mega Mozza Cheese Sticks exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for breaded fried mozzarella sticks, which is the closest approved source. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for breaded deep-fried mozzarella sticks (closest approved source); no Singapore-specific or branded entry was available in HPB Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this exact product. 2. Serving size of 240 g (12 × ~20 g sticks) is estimated from typical commercial product weights; actual product weight may vary. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on breaded fried snack foods with wheat-flour coating from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this product. GI 55 is classified as 'low'. 4. Halal status is listed as false as Potato Corner Singapore's halal certification status was not confirmed from an approved source at time of processing. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this food category; mozzarella cheese sticks contain no meaningful vitamin C. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.2 g/100 g reflects typical residual trans fat from deep-frying in partially hydrogenated oils; actual value may vary by oil used. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and all B-vitamins are estimated from USDA SR Legacy composite data and should be verified against the actual product label if available. 8. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03302114 is used as closest parent (mozzarella cheese food product); no dedicated FoodOn class for breaded fried mozzarella sticks was identified. 9. FoodEx2 code A0EJX (processed cheese) is used as closest parent; no dedicated FoodEx2 code for breaded fried mozzarella sticks was identified.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from scratch using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as adapted source. Added all mandatory multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases. Set GI to 55 (analogous estimate, medium) with University of Sydney GI Database basis. Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero. Used only allowed ontology match_type values (closest_parent).

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback. Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' for `glycemic_index` 55, and re-calculated `glycemic_load_per_serving` to 30.0. Updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `health_context.type2_diabetes.guidance` accordingly. Added the missing 'Zhi Shi Tiao' from `aliases_by_language.romanized` to the flat `aliases` array to ensure consistency. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 now pass.
